chunks.text: Laura Waller is a Professor at UC Berkeley EECS, holding the Ted Van Duzer Endowed Professorship and serving as Senior Fellow at Berkeley Institute of Data Science (BIDS). She leads the Computational Imaging Lab, developing optical imaging methods with joint optics and computational algorithms. Her affiliations include Bioengineering and Applied Sciences & Technology. Previously, she was a Postdoctoral Researcher and Physics Lecturer at Princeton University (2010-2012).

Laura Waller earned her BS (2004), MEng (2005), and PhD (2010) in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science from MIT. Her research areas include Physical Electronics (PHY), Signal Processing (SP), Biosystems & Computational Biology (BIO), and Graphics (GR), with specific focus on computational imaging and optics. She is affiliated with research centers like Berkeley Artificial Intelligence Research Lab (BAIR) and Integrated Modeling Process and Computation for Technology (IMPACT).

Laura Waller teaches EE 120: Signals and Systems, scheduled on Mondays and Wednesdays from 14:00-15:59 in Physics Building 4. Her contact information includes office at 514 Cory Hall, phone (510) 642-2753, email waller@berkeley.edu, with office hours by appointment. Administrative support is provided by Tracee Jackson (tmjackson@berkeley.edu).

Selected publications include works on computational imaging, optics, and microscopy, such as 'Computational imaging: Machine learning for 3D microscopy' in Nature (2015), 'Phase-space measurement and coherence synthesis of optical beams' in Nature Photonics (2012), and papers in Optica, IEEE ICCP, and Biomedical Optics Express. Topics cover lensless imaging, light-field microscopy, Fourier ptychographic microscopy, and phase imaging techniques.

Laura Waller has received numerous honors and awards, including Max Planck-Humboldt Medals (2024), AIMBE Fellow (2021), OSA Adolf Lomb Medal (2021), SPIE Early Career Achievement Award (2018), Carol D. Soc Distinguished Graduate Student Mentoring Award (2016), NSF CAREER award (2014), Moore Investigator in Data Driven Discovery (2014), Packard Fellow (2014), and Bakar Fellows Spark Award (2014). She is also a Chan-Zuckerberg Biohub Investigator.
